item.name: Airborne Assault Stage
item.description: June 6 AM GT only. The D-Day Airborne Assault occurs immediately before the beginning of the June 6 AM GT. Both sides conduct activity during this stage. Charts and tables are on the Airborne Assault Tables Card (AW (41.0)).
notes: The Airborne Assault Stage is NOT a solitaire exercise — both sides conduct movement and combat during this stage (AW (41.0)).
All DZs remain on the Battlefield Map until the June 10 AM GT (AW (41.1.0)).
After the Airborne Assault Stage, proceed to the Amphibious Assault Stage (AW (42.0)) before starting the normal June 6 AM GT.

subPhases.name: 1. Drop Zone Placement
subPhases.description: Establish Drop Zones (DZs) for each airborne division before drops begin.
subPhases.content: Before airborne units can start landing, players must establish **Drop Zones (DZs)** for each airborne division. All DZs remain on the Battlefield Map until the June 10 AM GT.
notes: Resolve Pegasus Bridge first — its success or failure affects 6th Airborne's opening position east of the Orne (AW (41.1.1)).
DZ scatter can shift your landing zones significantly — plan for contingencies if the DZ moves away from key objectives.

subPhases.name: 2. Airborne Drop
subPhases.description: Para units drop onto or near their DZs. Generate dummy units and resolve scatter.
subPhases.content: The Allied player begins airdropping his paratroop (Para) units onto the map at their respective DZs. All Para units are dropped as Coys, while HQs, artillery and air landing/Glider infantry land as Btns or half Btns. In each segment, conduct the full landing procedure for each DZ prior to moving to the next DZ.
notes: Unit scatter is rolled per individual para unit — some will land on the DZ, others may scatter significantly. This is the fog of war in action.
FoW dummy units are critical for confusing the German response — spread them to draw German forces away from your actual landing zones (AW (41.2.2)).

subPhases.name: 3. 1st Allied Movement
subPhases.description: Allied airborne units conduct their first movement. Stacking and FoW rules apply.
subPhases.content:
The Allied movement segment is conducted in the following order:
• Para Coy reorganization
• FoW unit re-group
• Movement.
notes: Use this first movement to consolidate scattered units toward objectives and DZs. Reorganization at DZ hexes can restore combat effectiveness (AW (41.3.4)).

subPhases.name: 4. Glider Landing
subPhases.description: Glider units land on or near DZs.
subPhases.content:
The Allied side now lands all scheduled Glider units on their respective DZs. Glider units do not have a FoW side and are always considered re-grouped.
Note: Some of the units show a "para" symbol. Treat all units scheduled to land during the Glider Landing Segment as glider units.
notes: Gliders carry heavier equipment (AT guns, light vehicles) that para units lack — getting them on target near the DZ is critical for holding objectives against German counterattacks.

subPhases.name: 5. 1st Allied Combat
subPhases.description: Allied airborne units may conduct combat against German units.
subPhases.content:
The Allied player may conduct attacks using any non-FoW units that are adjacent to enemy units. FoW units may not participate in an attack, however they do defend normally.
• Each German occupied hex may be attacked once per Allied Airborne Combat segment.
• A German occupied hex can be attacked from one friendly occupied hex only (i.e., Allied units from different hexes may not attack the same hex).
• In-battery artillery units that are adjacent to an enemy unit may add their barrage strength to the attack; however, the one attacking stack rule still applies.
• Each Allied unit may attack only one enemy occupied hex per segment.
• A unit cannot attack into or across prohibited terrain.
• Units may not attack across un-bridged river hexsides.
• All units in a hex must defend as one force (any overstacked units are ignored-owner's choice).
• All units in a hex do not have to attack, nor must they attack the same enemy hex.
notes: Early airborne combat is about seizing key positions (bridges, crossroads, battery positions) before the Germans can react in force.

subPhases.name: 6. 1st German Movement
subPhases.description: German units respond to the airborne landings with their first movement.
subPhases.content: German units have a limited ability to move during the Allied Airborne Stage.
notes: German response is limited by activation — not all units can react immediately. Focus on units closest to the DZs and key objectives.

subPhases.name: 7. 1st German Combat
subPhases.description: German units may conduct combat against Allied airborne units.
subPhases.content: The German player may conduct attacks against Allied units. The procedure is identical to the one listed in AW (41.5.0).
notes: German counterattacks during the Airborne Stage can eliminate isolated para units — the Allied player should avoid leaving small units exposed.

subPhases.name: 8. 2nd Allied Movement
subPhases.description: Allied airborne units conduct a second movement phase.
subPhases.content:
The Allied player may conduct movement using the same procedures outlined in the First Allied Movement Segment (AW (41.3.0)).
Exception: Units starting the movement segment in a Marsh hex may move one hex if not moving along a road.
notes: The second movement is your chance to adjust after seeing the German response — consolidate around objectives you can hold until the beach landings link up.

subPhases.name: 9. 2nd Allied Combat
subPhases.description: Allied airborne units conduct a second round of combat.
subPhases.content: The Allied player may conduct combat using the same procedures outlined in the First Allied Combat Segment (AW (41.5.0)).
notes: Use the second combat round to finish clearing objectives that survived the first assault — don't leave contested positions for the German second movement.

subPhases.name: 10. 2nd German Movement
subPhases.description: German units conduct a second movement in response to the airborne landings.
subPhases.content:
The German player may move any unit activated in the First German Movement Segment.
• Activated units may move up their full printed MA, however when entering a hex within three hexes of an Allied unit, multiply all MP costs by two.
• The German side may activate two additional units that are within six hexes of a CW unit and two units that are within six hexes of a US unit.
notes: German second movement can bring reinforcements from further away — watch for counterattack buildups around your key objectives.

subPhases.name: 11. 2nd German Combat
subPhases.description: German units conduct a second round of combat. After this, the Airborne Assault Stage ends.
subPhases.content: The German player may conduct attacks against Allied units. The procedure is identical to the one listed in AW (41.6.0).
notes: This is the final segment of the Airborne Stage — after resolution, airborne positions are set for the beginning of the beach invasion.
From June 6 PM onward, glider reinforcements arrive at the start of Allied Movement Phase — plan DZ access routes for incoming gliders (AW (41.12.0)).

item.content:
The D-Day Airborne Assault is simulated by the **Airborne Assault Stage** that occurs immediately before the beginning of the June 6 AM GT. The Airborne Assault Stage is not a solitaire exercise, both sides conduct activity during this stage. The charts and tables required for this module are found on the Airborne Assault Tables Card. There are 11 segments in this stage and they are conducted in the following order:
1. Drop Zone Placement Segment
2. Airborne Drop Segment
3. 1st Allied Movement Segment
4. Glider Landing Segment
5. 1st Allied Combat Segment
6. 1st German Movement Segment
7. 1st German Combat Segment
8. 2nd Allied Movement Segment
9. 2nd Allied Combat Segment
10. 2nd German Movement Segment
11. 2nd German Combat Segment

item.name: Amphibious Assault Stage
item.description: Conducted prior to the June 6 AM GT (after the Airborne Assault Stage), and then prior to each subsequent GT until all beaches are fully cleared. There are three sub-phases, each with 8 segments (AW (42.0)).
notes: Normally there will be two Amphibious Stages: June 6 AM GT and June 6 PM GT. If all beaches are not cleared by the end of June 6 PM, continue each successive GT until cleared (AW (42.4.0)).
The first sub-phase of June 6 AM has three EXTRA segments before the Landing Segment: Pointe du Hoc Assault, Utah Beach Drift, and DD Tank Segments (AW (42.4.0)).
Naval units are NOT available for NGS on June 6 — they are supporting the landings. NGS available starting June 7 AM or when all beaches are cleared (AW (38.3.0)).

subPhases.name: Special Segments (1st Sub-Phase Only)
subPhases.description: June 6 AM GT, 1st sub-phase only: Pointe du Hoc, Utah Drift, and DD Tanks are resolved before the regular landing segments.
subPhases.content:
The US player places the Pointe du Hoc assault force (two ranger invasion units denoted on the Amphibious Assault Diagram) in hex C6402.
• To determine if the guns are actually in place, the German side rolls 1d10.
  • If the result is greater than "zero," the StP is flipped over to its reverse side (without the guns).
  • If the result is "zero" the guns are in place and the StP is treated normally.
• During the June 6 AM GT Allied Movement Phase, the rangers must assault the StP.
• The US player rolls 1d10 for each step of rangers (each ranger unit has two steps).
  • For each result of 0-3, the StP takes one hit. If the StP guns are in place, one hit destroys the guns (flip the StP to its reverse side) and a second hit eliminates the StP. If the guns are not in place, one hit eliminates the StP.
• The German side then rolls 1d10 for each step of rangers.
  • For each result of 0-2, one step of rangers is eliminated.
• If the StP survives the assault, the US player may make a second assault with any surviving ranger steps during the June 6 PM GT.
• If the StP survives both assaults, the ranger units are eliminated.
• If the StP is eliminated in either GT, the ranger units remain in the hex. They may not conduct any action except defend. Each surviving step counts as one standard step and has a defensive strength of one, and a PR of eight. They cannot convert discretionary hits into retreats, nor can they retreat after a FS mission.
• If a US unit of any size enters the hex, the units are immediately removed. The US player receives one step of Rangers that may be added to any on map Ranger Btn or used to reconstitute an eliminated Ranger Btn with one step. The replacement step arrives during the next Friendly Replacement Segment.
notes: Pointe du Hoc is a high-risk, high-reward assault — the Rangers must scale cliffs under fire to neutralize German guns threatening the beaches (AW (42.5.0)).
Utah Beach drift is historical — the landing force came ashore about 2km south of the planned beach, which ironically was less defended (AW (42.6.0)).
DD tanks in rough seas have a significant chance of sinking — their loss removes critical armor support for the first waves (AW (42.7.0)).

subPhases.name: Seg 1: Landing Segment
subPhases.description: Allied invasion units land on beach displays. Place units in appropriate landing boxes.
subPhases.content: Place all units scheduled to land during the current sub-phase on the Display in the Sea Boxes of the sub-sector they are scheduled to land in.
notes: Units land in waves — not everything arrives in the first sub-phase. Check the landing schedule for which units arrive in each sub-phase.

subPhases.name: Seg 2: German Fire Segment
subPhases.description: German defensive fire against units on the beach displays.
subPhases.content:
German Fire is resolved against each 'in range' box containing an Allied unit. Fire is resolved in the following sequence:
• Each Allied occupied box may only be fired on once per sub-phase.
• Each German unit may fire at every Allied occupied box that is in range of the unit.
• All German units that can fire into an Allied occupied box must combine their fire strength into one fire.
• Start in the Beach Box Row, working across each beach, until all eligible units in the Beach Box Row have been fired upon.
• Then move up one row, to the Shingle Box Row, moving across each beach until all eligible units in the Shingle Box Row have been fired upon.
• Continue this process (moving up one row) until all eligible Allied units have been fired upon.
notes: German defensive fire is strongest in the first sub-phase before Allied units can advance off the beach — Omaha Beach is historically the most heavily defended.

subPhases.name: Seg 3: Allied Fire Segment
subPhases.description: Allied naval gunfire and direct fire support against German beach defenses.
subPhases.content: The Allied player conducts Naval Gun Fire (if available) and then any eligible unit fire.
notes: Naval gunfire is your primary advantage on the beaches — concentrate it on the strongest German defensive positions to create gaps for the assault.

subPhases.name: Seg 4: Allied Movement
subPhases.description: Allied units on beach displays advance toward German positions.
subPhases.content: Unpinned Allied units may move from box to box on the Displays. Units may move from one box to an adjacent box (AW (42.1.0)). The movement may be through horizontal, vertical, and diagonal boundaries. The movement allowance for all units is described in AW (42.2.1).
notes: Getting units off the beach and inland is the priority — units stuck on the beach are vulnerable to German fire each sub-phase.

subPhases.name: Seg 5: German Movement
subPhases.description: German units on beach displays may reposition.
subPhases.content:
German infantry units on the Display must be checked for activation.
• German infantry units in Beach Display Boxes are considered active if they have conducted either defensive fire, assault fire, or been fired on in any way (including assault and naval gunfire).
• Certain Beach Displays start with German units not yet on the Display but ready to enter depending upon a reaction DR for each unit. Each Display has an image of a German unit that may enter (this image is printed next to the Exit Boxes the unit is placed in) and the DR needed for it to be activated. No reaction DR is made on the AM Invasion Stage First or Second Sub-phase.
• If the German player moves other German units (other than the above units) onto the Display, then the solitaire mode for that display ends and the German player becomes active. He is then free to move his own units without regard to the restrictions in AW (42.12.1).
• If activated, the German player may voluntarily withdraw units from the Beach Displays if they are in Beach Exit Box. Convert each unit to a breakdown 1-2-6 infantry Coy and place it on the Battlefield Map in the hex noted on the Beach Exit Box they occupy.
notes: German repositioning can plug gaps in the defense — watch for German units shifting to cover beach exits you're trying to breach.

subPhases.name: Seg 6: Assault Combat
subPhases.description: Allied units assault German defensive positions on the beach.
subPhases.content: If enemy units occupy the same box, assault combat must take place. If solitaire mode is still in place, the Allied player will conduct both the Allied and German assault fire. Each assault must be fully resolved before an assault in another box can be initiated. All fire is simultaneous. Do not inflict step losses or retreats until both sides have resolved their fire.
notes: Assault combat on the beach uses special beach assault rules — not standard GOSS GA. Concentrate force on key defensive positions guarding beach exits.

subPhases.name: Seg 7: Demolition
subPhases.description: Engineer units attempt to clear beach obstacles and open exits.
subPhases.content: The Allied player attempts to clear beach obstacles and breach Seawalls/Draws.
notes: Clearing beach exits is essential for getting vehicles and heavy equipment off the beach — engineer demolition is the key to this.

subPhases.name: Seg 8: Pin Recovery
subPhases.description: Pinned units attempt to recover. After all segments, check for Beach Area Clearance.
subPhases.content: Pin markers are removed from all units (both sides) at this time. During medium and high tide conditions, pinned units occupying a Beach Row Box do not remove their pin marker; instead they keep the marker in place and immediately move from the Beach Box Row into the Shingle Box Row of the same sub-sector.
notes: Pin recovery is critical — pinned units can't advance or assault, and they're vulnerable to German fire. Prioritize recovering units near beach exits.
Beach clearance is checked after ALL segments — once a beach is cleared, it transitions to normal reinforcement arrival. Uncleared beaches repeat the full Amphibious Stage next GT (AW (42.16.0)).

item.content: The **Amphibious Invasion Stage** is conducted prior to the June 6 AM GT, and then prior to the start of each GT thereafter until all beaches are fully cleared. There are three sub-phases in each stage.

modifications.gate: AW: ENA is NOT used in Atlantic Wall. Ignore all references to Extended Night Activity.

patch.appendContent:
### Sequence of Play (SoP) (31.1.0)

Ignore GOSS (3.2.0) thru (3.5.0) and (9.0). Much of the activity of a regular GT has been abstracted out of the introductory scenarios. The regular SoP has been modified with some phases deleted entirely. The below sequence of play is used in the first three scenarios.

### Sequence of Play (Intermediate) (35.1.0)

When playing the intermediate scenarios, all phases of the SoP are used however some sub-phases may be skipped or modified. Changes to the GT sequence are noted below.
appendNotes: Check your scenario tier to know which SoP modifications apply — introductory scenarios strip out most logistics and command phases, while intermediate and full scenarios progressively add complexity.

patch.appendContent:
### Air Points & Naval Units (Intermediate) (35.4.0)

Each Scenario will outline the use and availability of APs and naval units.

### Air Points (38.2.0)

Ignore all rules applying to Strategic Map assignment, air supply mission, and Strategic Interdiction.
appendNotes: Allied air superiority is a major advantage in AW — the Luftwaffe has very limited AP. Use Allied air primarily for GS and GI to support the breakout from the beachhead.

modifications.gate: AW: All scenarios use simplified logistics — no full Logistics Table. ADV, AmP, fuel, and TP rules vary by scenario tier (AW (35.5.0)).

patch.appendContent:
### Logistics (Intermediate) (35.5.0)

Each Scenario will list logistic rules that apply, however in all intermediate scenarios or in all campaign games that players do not use the full logistic rules; apply the below rules. In all cases, GOSS GenS rules apply unless specifically stated otherwise.

### Allied Fuel (35.5.1)

Allied Mech unit printed MA is reduced as follows:
• June 7 AM to June 11 AM GT: -4.
• June 12 AM to July 2 AM GT: -2.
• July 3 AM GT to end of scenario: all Mech units have their full printed MA.
Note: Reduce unit MA for ground conditions, GenS status and Exploit movement after reducing for the above.

### German Fuel (35.5.2)

The following movement restrictions are in effect for both the friendly Movement and Exploitation Phases.
• Beginning on the June 9 AM GT the German side must secretly designate one GT as a "low fuel" GT. On that GT, German Mech units subtract two from their printed MA.
• During the remaining two GTs of that GD all Mech units may move a maximum of one-half their printed MA and may only move one hex while in PA mode.
• Each GD the German side may select a number of formations or BG to operate with their full MA.
• Until the July 3 AM GT, one formation, or four independent BGs may operate with their full MA.
• Beginning the July 4 AM GT, two division formations or eight independent BG may operate with their full MA.
Note: Reduce unit MA for ground conditions, GenS status and Exploit movement after reducing for the above.

### Ammo Depletion (Intermediate) (35.5.3)

Scenario instructions will outline each side's ADV. Adjust army and corps ADV based on the number of formations assigned and the use of AmP. AmP arrival is dictated in each scenario's rules. Do not use the Logistics Table.

### Depots (35.5.4)

Depots may be moved and/or new depots may be activated. On the GD and for the two following GDs, that a depot is moved and/or activated; all Mech units subordinate to that army reduce their printed MA by two. Use the procedures outlined in GOSS (15.8.2) & AW (15.9.0). Exception: Ignore TP and FP requirements.
appendNotes: Allied fuel restrictions (-4 MA early, -2 mid-game) severely limit Mech mobility in the beachhead phase — plan offensives around when fuel improves (AW (35.5.1)).
The German low-fuel mechanic is secret — the Allied player never knows which GT the German chose. This adds significant uncertainty to German counterattack timing (AW (35.5.2)).
Depot activation penalties (-2 MA for 3 GDs) mean you should plan depot moves well in advance of planned offensives (AW (35.5.4)).

patch.appendContent:
### Allied Fuel (35.5.1)

Allied Mech unit printed MA is reduced as follows:
• June 7 AM to June 11 AM GT: -4.
• June 12 AM to July 2 AM GT: -2.
• July 3 AM GT to end of scenario: all Mech units have their full printed MA.
Note: Reduce unit MA for ground conditions, GenS status and Exploit movement after reducing for the above.

### German Fuel (35.5.2)

The following movement restrictions are in effect for both the friendly Movement and Exploitation Phases.
• Beginning on the June 9 AM GT the German side must secretly designate one GT as a "low fuel" GT. On that GT, German Mech units subtract two from their printed MA.
• During the remaining two GTs of that GD all Mech units may move a maximum of one-half their printed MA and may only move one hex while in PA mode.
• Each GD the German side may select a number of formations or BG to operate with their full MA.
• Until the July 3 AM GT, one formation, or four independent BGs may operate with their full MA.
• Beginning the July 4 AM GT, two division formations or eight independent BG may operate with their full MA.
Note: Reduce unit MA for ground conditions, GenS status and Exploit movement after reducing for the above.
appendNotes: AW replaces GOSS fuel tracking with direct MA penalties — no fuel points to manage, but the penalties are significant early in the campaign.

patch.appendContent:
### Fire Support (FS) Missions (31.9.0)

Unlike in the standard GOSS rules FS mission types are consolidated into one FS mission for each target hex. Only one FS mission is allowed in a single target hex. Each mission may consist of multiple volleys. Ammo depletion DRs only effect artillery mission point availability.
• Coy sized units may only observe for one FS mission.
• Btn sized units may observe for two FS missions.
• Air, naval and artillery mission points are totaled into one mission. This total is used to determine the strength and number of volleys in each mission.

### Naval Unit Availability (11.9.0)

Only the Allied side has naval units. The Allied side determines the number and type of naval units available for the current GD as follows:
• There are five types of naval units available as shown at the top of the Naval Unit Availability chart. The five types are: Battleship (BB), Monitor (MN), Heavy Cruiser (CA), Light Cruiser (CL), and Destroyer (DD).
• Below each type are two numbers or dashes separated by a slash. Left of the slash is for the western task force (US). Right is for the eastern task force (CW).
• On the left of the chart is one column representing DR results from 0 to 9.
• Each nationality rolls 1d10 for each type of naval unit. The result is the number of naval units of that type available to that task force.
• Beginning the July 1 GD, the Western TF loses all naval units except for DD.
• Beginning the July 8 GD, the Eastern TF may have a maximum of 1 BB or 1 Mon, 1 CA, 2 CL and 4 DD. The CW player rolls normally, however Eastern TF may not have more naval units than listed above.
• Record the number and type of naval units available for that GD on the AP Track Record.
• Scenario instructions may set limits on number of naval units available.
• Naval units are subject to ammunition depletion. Their ADV is four; this value may not be modified. If a naval unit fails to pass the AD check, it may not be used again that GD. This check does not affect availability for following GDs.

### Naval Gunfire Support (38.3.0)

Naval units are not available on June 6 (they are supporting the landings).
• Starting June 7 AM GT or on the first GT after all beaches are cleared use AW (11.9.0).
• If any beach is not cleared of German units on the June 7 AM GT, the AG that has any beach that is not cleared does not roll for naval units. Each GT until all beaches are cleared, that AG NGS support on any not cleared beach uses the same procedures as called for in AW (42.0), the June 6 PM GT. NGS is not available on the June 6 Night GT.
appendNotes: Naval gunfire is a massive force multiplier in AW — each battleship and cruiser provides significant FS points that don't require ammo tracking. Use NGS on the toughest German positions.

patch.appendContent:
### Fieldworks (FW) (31.10.0)

Initial scenario setup will detail where FW are placed at the beginning of the scenario, and may include ET-3s. All rules pertaining to the construction and benefits of FWs are in effect.
Exception: ET-3s may not be built.
appendNotes: ET-3 construction is prohibited in introductory scenarios — only pre-placed fieldworks are available (AW (31.10.0)).

patch.appendContent:
### Movement Halts (31.8.0)

Ignore GOSS (7.3.3a) & (7.3.3b), movement halts occur when:
• A unit in PA mode moves adjacent to an enemy unit.
• A unit enters a hex that is adjacent to an enemy unit and the hex does not contain movement covering terrain.
Movement halts generated by Forts and ETs are still in effect (GOSS (7.3.3c)).
**Designer's Note:** in the more advanced rules, the requirement for artillery is back in effect. I wanted to initially simplify the creation of a movement halt in these easier scenarios — besides most hexes would be in range of artillery in these situations anyway.

### Exploitation Movement (31.5.0)

At the beginning of a friendly Movement Phase, Mech units may be placed in Exploit mode. Ignore all references to Exploitation Phases, Maneuver Reserve, and Exploit Mode.
• Units in Exploit mode may not move in the Movement Phase.
• In the Combat Phase after all GAs have been resolved, Units in Exploit mode may move up to one-half of their printed MA and if able conduct an overrun attack (GOSS (7.11.0)).
• Remove the Exploit marker once the unit has completed movement and overruns.
• Units in Exploit mode do not pay the extra MP for moving adjacent to enemy units, unless there is no movement covering terrain in that hex.
appendNotes: AW introductory scenarios simplify movement halts — no artillery requirement, just adjacency to enemy in open terrain. This makes defensive positions in covering terrain much stronger (AW (31.8.0)).

modifications.gate: AW Intro: AP available per scenario. Ignore Strategic Map air rules. No air transport. Ignore all references to Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammo points, ENA, and bridge collapse (AW (31.0)).

appendNotes: Introductory scenarios dramatically simplify air — just use the AP listed in your scenario setup. No strategic air, no air supply, no ATP.

modifications.gate: AW Intro: Ignore GOSS (3.2.0) through (3.5.0) and (9.0). Command phase functions are abstracted — Allied player only designates movement (AW (31.1.0)).

appendNotes: Most command functions are removed in introductory scenarios — no corps/army assignments, no rest GTs, no lull determination. Focus on movement and combat (AW (31.1.0)).

modifications.gate:
**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.11.0)):** Ignore all references to ammunition and fuel unless stated otherwise in the scenario instructions. Scenario rules will describe if and how to trace GenS. HQ and units unable to trace GenS are marked with an "on hand supply" (OhS) marker. The unit retains the "OhS" marker until the unit:
• Is able to trace GenS path of the appropriate length.
• Uses more than one half of its MA in one Movement Phase.
• Contributes FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Participates as an attacking unit in a GA.
• Participates as a defending unit in a GA and does not halve its current combat strength.
If a unit marked with an "OhS" marker does any one of the above, it is immediately marked with an "out of supply" (OoS) marker.
Units that are OoS are affected as follows:
• Units may not enter PA or Exploit mode.
• Unit's MA is halved.
• Units may not contribute FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Units participating in a GA (attacking or defending) have their current combat strength halved.

**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.0)):** The first three scenarios are designed to introduce players to the basic game concepts. Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. HQ and units unable to trace GenS are marked with an "on hand supply" (OhS) marker. The unit retains the "OhS" marker until the unit:
• Is able to trace GenS path of the appropriate length.

• Contributes FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Participates as an attacking unit in a GA.
• Participates as a defending unit in a GA and does not halve its current combat strength.
If a unit marked with an "OhS" marker does any one of the above, it is immediately marked with an "out of supply" (OoS) marker.
Units that are OoS are affected as follows:
• Units may not enter PA or Exploit mode.
• Unit's MA is halved.
• Units may not contribute FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Units participating in a GA (attacking or defending) have their current combat strength halved.

If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.0)):** The first three scenarios are designed to introduce players to the basic game concepts. Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

patch.appendContent:
### Scenario One: Operation Goodwood (32.0)

This scenario is the first of three introductory scenarios. It is a quarter map scenario and depicts Monty's mid-July battle to capture Caen, and drive towards Falaise. The scenario is confined to the area on map "C" shown by the thick dashed line on the scenario diagram. Units that retreat out of this area may not reenter play.

### Scenario Length (32.1.0)

The scenario begins on the July 18 AM GT and ends with the completion of the July 19 PM GT or the July 20 Night GT. All special scenario rules apply no matter which scenario end point is chosen. If players choose the shorter version, ignore the German reinforcements listed for the July 19 PM GT. Playing time for the short version is approximately two to two and one-half hours, for the longer version approximately three to four hours.

### Air Points (AP) (32.2.0)

Only the Allied side has AP available. In addition to GS and GI missions, the Allied side has a special Carpet Bombing mission that is made prior to starting the scenario. Carpet Bombing does not require the use of AP.

### Available Air Points (32.2.1)

Beginning the July 18 PM GT, the Allied side has 32 AP available each GD.
**Designer's note:** Allied AP are factored into the carpet bombing sequence on July 18 AM GT.

### Carpet Bombing Mission (32.2.2)

Prior to beginning the July 18 AM GT the Allied side conducts a special carpet bombing mission. All German units within the area bounded by 5120-5125 to 5625-5620 (inclusive) are attacked by this mission.
• Each German occupied hex within the above area receives one AS hit (mark with blue AS marker) and is affected as follows:
• Roll 1d10 for each German-occupied hex, apply applicable DRMs, and implement the results. All DRMs are cumulative:
• -1, if there are two or less steps in the hex.
• -1, if at least one pure AFV unit is in the hex.
• -1, if there is an ET-2 in the hex.
• -2, if there is an ET-3 in the hex.
Cross reference the modified DR below and apply the results noted to the right.
• 0: No effect
• 1-3: one-step loss
• 4-6: two-step losses
• 7-9: three-step losses
• All units that suffered the carpet bombing attack (even if no step losses suffered) are affected as follows:
• Units in that hex do not generate movement halts (even if in an ET-2 or ET-3).
• Any unit that moves out of one of the effected hexes retains the blue AS marker and has their MA halved.
• At the end of the July 18 AM German Player Turn the German player conducts a defensive PR check for each unit with a blue AS marker. Units that pass may remove the marker.
• All remaining AS markers from this attack are removed at the end of the July 18 PM German Player Turn.

### Naval Gunfire Support (32.3.0)

Naval Gunfire is factored into the available artillery mission points. There are no specific naval FS mission points available.

### Artillery (32.5.0)

The Allied side receives four artillery groups that represent the four AGRAs and all the naval gun fire support committed to this battle. Allied divisional artillery is represented by the divisional HQ units. Two of the artillery groups can only support units of the II Canadian Corps, and the other two can only support units of the I or VIII Corps. The Allied player should designate which groups are supporting which corps. These designations may not change during the course of the scenario.
Note: Players should use two-artillery Btn counters from four AGRAs provided with the game and one-artillery Btn from each division. One-artillery Btn represents the AGRA on the map and the other is placed on the army track to represent the number of FS mission points available to that AGRA. The divisional artillery Btn is used on the track in the same manner as the AGRA Btn.

### Allied Artillery Groups (32.5.1)

The Allied side receives four artillery groups that represent the four AGRAs and all the naval gun fire support committed to this battle.

### Allied FS Mission Points (32.5.1a)

All Allied mission points are available each GT. Reset the number of available mission points at the beginning of each GT. Each group is assigned a number of mission points and a range.
• Each Artillery group: 16 points, range 7.
• Each division HQ: 12 points, range 6.

### German Artillery Groups (32.5.2)

The German side does not use artillery groups or divisional HQ to represent their artillery.

### German FS Mission Points (32.5.2a)

Each German artillery unit may conduct one FS mission each GT. When an artillery unit fires, place an "Ammo Depleted" marker on the unit. Remove all Ammo Depleted markers at the end of the GT.
• Artillery units can only move if out-of-battery and only fire if in-battery.
• Artillery units use their printed barrage and MA.
• Nebelwerfer FS mission DRMs are in effect.
• German Heavy AA Coys may conduct FS missions.

### Nebelwerfer (NW) Units (32.5.2b)

NW units may fire once per GD, using their printed barrage strength and range. The DRMs for NW units are in effect.
• They may contribute to or initiate a FS mission in support of any unit.
• They may be in or out of battery and operate in all other aspects as regular artillery units.
Note: Players should mark these units with "ammo depletion" markers when they participate in a barrage, remove the marker at the beginning of the AM GT.

### German Heavy Flak Units (32.5.2c)

German heavy flak Coys may contribute mission points to a FS mission.
• Each Coy has 1 point and a range of 4.
• Heavy Flak Coys may fire once each player turn.

### Weather Conditions (32.4.0)

Atmospheric and Ground conditions for each GT are as follows:
• July 18 AM, PM & Night: Clear and dry.
• July 19 AM, PM & Night: POvr and wet.
• July 20 PM: Ovr and wet.
• July 20 PM and Night: Ovr and mud.

### Movement (32.7.0)

There are no transport points (TP) available. Allied divisions do not have the inherent TP capability nor may units mount or dismount ((7.8.0) does not apply). Neither side may use Strat Movement.

### Allied Movement Restrictions (32.7.1)

Units of the 2nd Canadian Division, the 2nd Canadian Armored Brigade and the two artillery counters representing II Canadian Corps artillery may NOT voluntarily move east of the 2nd Canadian division boundary shown on the scenario diagram. If these units are forced to retreat east of the line, they must move back to their operating area in next Allied Movement Phase. They may not enter PA mode or be designated to make a tactical assault until such time as they are east of the designated line.

### German Movement Restrictions (32.7.2)

Only those German units west of the 49th British Division eastern boundary may move or conduct offensive GAs. If attacked they defend normally and may observe for and conduct FS missions. Pz Coys of the 12th SS Division are exempt from the above restrictions.

### Bridges (32.6.0)

Except as noted below, bridges may not be demolished or constructed. Ignore all rules pertaining to heavy bridges or bridge collapse.

### Bridge Construction (32.6.1)

At the end of the July 19 AM GT (only), if a hex south of the Orne River is occupied by an Allied unit and if that hex began the scenario occupied by a German unit, the Allied player may place a bridge across one river hexside at that location. This means that no more than one bridge can cross the Orne River into a single hex, however as many bridges as there are hexes on the south side of the river, and that meet the criteria above can be placed.
• The Allied player may place up to three such bridges, however only one bridge may be place each GT.

modifications.gate:
**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.11.0)):** Ignore all references to ammunition and fuel unless stated otherwise in the scenario instructions. Scenario rules will describe if and how to trace GenS. HQ and units unable to trace GenS are marked with an "on hand supply" (OhS) marker. The unit retains the "OhS" marker until the unit:
• Is able to trace GenS path of the appropriate length.
• Uses more than one half of its MA in one Movement Phase.
• Contributes FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Participates as an attacking unit in a GA.
• Participates as a defending unit in a GA and does not halve its current combat strength.
If a unit marked with an "OhS" marker does any one of the above, it is immediately marked with an "out of supply" (OoS) marker.
Units that are OoS are affected as follows:
• Units may not enter PA or Exploit mode.
• Unit's MA is halved.
• Units may not contribute FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Units participating in a GA (attacking or defending) have their current combat strength halved.

**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.0)):** The first three scenarios are designed to introduce players to the basic game concepts. Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.0)):** The first three scenarios are designed to introduce players to the basic game concepts. Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

**Introductory Scenario (AW (31.0)):** The first three scenarios are designed to introduce players to the basic game concepts. Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

patch.appendContent:
### Scenario Two: Operation Epsom "The Hill of Death" (33.0)

This quarter map scenario depicts Monty's late June attempt to take Caen by a flanking maneuver that would take the commanding height of Hill 112. Hill 112 eventually became known as the "Hill of Kalvarienberg" to the Germans, among other names used to signify the horrific combat occurring on this position. The German command viewed this position as the lynchpin of the defense of Caen and the road to Falaise. The scenario is confined to the area on map C shown by the thick dashed line on the scenario diagram. The eastern, southern, and western edges of the play area are all German-controlled. Units that retreat out of this area may not re-enter play.

### Scenario Length (Epsom) (33.1.0)

The scenario begins on the June 25 AM GT and lasts until the end of the June 30 Night GT. Playing time for Scenario Two is approximately four hours.

### Air Points (Epsom) (33.2.0)

Only the Allied side has AP available. They have 24 AP available from the start of the scenario.

### Naval Gunfire Support (Epsom) (33.3.0)

The Allied side has the use of one BB and one CA.
• The BB has eight FS mission points.
• The CA has six FS mission points.
• Only one naval unit may participate in a single FS mission.
• Each Naval unit is available once each GD (AM or PM GT).
• Any hex within five hexes of C3620 is within range of either naval unit.

### Artillery (Epsom) (33.5.0)

The Allied side receives one artillery group to represent the British VIII Corps Artillery. Use the VIII Corps HQ counter to represent the group on the map. The VIII Corps ADV marker can be used to track FS mission points available.

### FS Mission Point Availability (Epsom) (33.5.1a)

Allied FS mission points are available each player turn. Reset the number of available mission points at the end of a player turn. Each group is assigned a number of mission points and a range.
• VIII artillery group: 16 points, range 6.
• Division HQ: 12 points, range 6.

### German Artillery Groups (Epsom) (33.5.2)

The German side does not use artillery groups or divisional HQ to represent their artillery.

### German FS Mission Points (Epsom) (33.5.2a)

Each German artillery unit may conduct one FS mission each GT. When an artillery unit fires, place an "Ammo Depleted" marker on the unit. Remove all Ammo Depleted markers at the end of the GT.
• Artillery units can only move if out-of-battery and only fire if in-battery.
• Artillery units use their printed barrage and MA.
• Nebelwerfer FS mission DRMs are in effect.
• German Heavy AA Coys may conduct FS missions.

### Weather Conditions (Epsom) (33.4.0)

Atmospheric and Ground conditions for each GT are as follows:
• June 25 AM, PM & Night: POvr and dry.
• June 26 AM, PM & Night: POvr and wet.
• June 27 AM: POvr and dry.
• June 27 PM & Night: clear and dry.
• June 28 AM, PM & Night: clear and dry.
• June 29 AM, PM & Night: clear and dry.
• June 30 AM: clear and dry.
• June 30 PM & Night: POvr and wet.

### Movement (Epsom) (33.7.0)

There are no transport points (TP) available. Allied divisions do not have the inherent TP capability nor may units mount or dismount ((7.8.0) does not apply). Neither side may use Strat movement.

### Allied Movement Restrictions (Epsom) (33.7.1)

• All units of the VIII Corps are inactive until the June 26 AM GT. No unit of the VIII Corps may move, perform construction, observe for or conduct a FS mission, or conduct an offensive GA. Beginning the June 26 AM GT all units operate normally.
• Except for the 15th Infantry Division, no unit of the VIII Corps may move west of the 49th Infantry Division boundary.
• 49th Infantry Division may not move east of the division boundary line shown on the scenario diagram.

### German Movement Restrictions (Epsom) (33.7.2)

Only those German units west of the 49th British Division eastern boundary may move or conduct offensive GAs. If attacked they defend normally and may observe for and conduct FS missions. Pz Coys of the 12th SS Division are exempt from the above restrictions.

### Bridges (Epsom) (33.6.0)

Bridge demolition is conducted at the end of the friendly Movement Phase or as a result of hasty demolition ((17.3.3a) & (17.3.3b)). Ignore all rules pertaining to heavy bridges or bridge collapse.

### Bridge Construction (Epsom) (33.6.1)

At the beginning of any GT that the Allied side has a unit on the south side of the Odon River, he may:
• Place one bridge marker in one Allied occupied hex on the south side of the Odon River.
• The Allied player may place up to three such bridges, however only one bridge may be place each GT.

modifications.gate:

HQ and units unable to trace GenS are marked with an "on hand supply" (OhS) marker. The unit retains the "OhS" marker until the unit:
• Is able to trace GenS path of the appropriate length.

• Participates as a defending unit in a GA and does not halve its current combat strength.
If a unit marked with an "OhS" marker does any one of the above, it is immediately marked with an "out of supply" (OoS) marker.
Units that are OoS are affected as follows:
• Units may not enter PA or Exploit mode.
• Unit's MA is halved.
• Units may not contribute FS mission points to a FS mission.
• Units participating in a GA (attacking or defending) have their current combat strength halved.

Many game functions are either not used or are modified to facilitate ease of play. If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

If there is any conflict between a rule in either rulebook and those presented here, the rules presented in the scenario take precedence. All references to; Maneuver Reserve, fuel, ammunition points, extended night activity and bridge collapse are ignored.

patch.appendContent:
### Scenario Three: The Battle for Cherbourg (34.0)

This scenario represents the VII US Corps' ten-day long battle to clear the Cherbourg Peninsula and capture the vital port of Cherbourg. The US First Army has just cut the Cotentin Peninsula the day before and the stage was now set for the drive north. The scenario is played on map A. The area north of the red start line is under German control. The area on or south of the line is US controlled.

### Scenario Length (Cherbourg) (34.1.0)

The scenario begins on the June 19 AM GT and ends with the completion of the June 30 Night GT. Playing time for Scenario Three is approximately four to five hours.

### Air Points (Cherbourg) (34.2.0)

Only the Allied side has AP available.

### Available Air Points (Cherbourg) (34.2.1)

The Allied side has 36 AP available each GD.

### Naval Gunfire Support (Cherbourg) (34.3.0)

The Allied Side has one BB, one CA, two CL, and three DD available. GOSS (11.3.2), (11.4.2) & AW (11.9.0) apply with the following exceptions:
• Each Naval unit is available during each AM and PM GT. Do not roll for Ammo Depletion.
• Range is traced from any full sea hex.
• Naval units are able to support FS missions as detailed in AW (31.9.0) & (31.9.3).
• If the Allied side allocates one naval unit to a German WN or StP with barrage capabilities, that WN or StP FS mission points are reduced to zero for that GT.

### Artillery (Cherbourg) (34.5.0)

Both side's artillery groups are not used. Both side's artillery units are now used. See AW (4.5.9) & (4.5.10) for rules regarding StP and WN. See AW (31.9.4) for determining FS mission capacity.

### Ammo Depletion (Cherbourg) (34.5.3)

GOSS (11.8.0) Applies; ignore all rules pertaining to ammo points, split fire, NW and Flak ammo depletion rules in GOSS or AW. Ignore all rules pertaining to ADV modification.

### Allied Ammo Depletion (Cherbourg) (34.5.3a)

US side has an ADV of six.

### German Ammo Depletion (Cherbourg) (34.5.3b)

German ADV varies depending on GD.
• Start to the June 20 Night GT: 4
• June 21 AM to the June 24 Night GT: 3
• June 25 AM GT to end: 2

### Weather Conditions (Cherbourg) (34.4.0)

The weather on the June 19 AM, PM and Night GTs is POvr with dry ground conditions. Starting with the June 20 AM GT the Allied side must determine atmospheric and ground conditions each GT. Roll 1d10 to first determine the atmospheric condition.
• 0-4, atmospheric condition is clear.
• 5-6, atmospheric condition is POvr.
• 7-9, atmospheric condition is Ovr.
If the result indicates either POvr or Ovr, roll a second 1d10 to determine ground condition.
• 0-3 ground condition is dry.
• 4-6 ground condition is wet.
• 7-9 ground condition is mud.

### Movement (Cherbourg) (34.7.0)

There are no transport points (TP) available. Allied divisions do have their inherent TP capability. Neither side may use Strat movement.

### Allied Movement Restrictions (Cherbourg) (34.7.1)

There are no restrictions.

### German Movement Restrictions (Cherbourg) (34.7.2)

Due to the Allied ground interdiction capability; that is out of the scope of this scenario, German movement is restricted as follows:
• During all Night GTs or any GT in which the atmospheric condition is overcast, German units may move up to their full MA.
• During AM and PM GTs if the atmospheric condition is partial overcast all German Mech units subtract two from their MA and German Leg units subtract one.
• During AM and PM GTs if the atmospheric condition is clear, all German unit's MA is reduced by one-third.

### Cherbourg Morale (34.7.3)

See AW (1.2.6e) for effects of Cherbourg morale.

### Replacements (Cherbourg) (34.10.0)

GOSS (22.5.0) (Unit Consolidation) applies. WN and StP may not be consolidated into infantry or artillery units.

### Allied Replacements (Cherbourg) (34.10.1)

At the start of any one AM GT, the US side may declare a GD lull. The lull goes into effect immediately and lasts until the beginning of the next AM GT. Lull effects are as follows:
• Allied units may only move one hex each GT.
• German units may move up to one-third of their printed MA.
• Units that start adjacent to an enemy unit may only move so as to move closer to their source of GenS.
• Neither side may initiate any type of combat.
• All US Btns that have at least one step loss may replace one step. Players should keep all units (including breakdown Coys) separate as Btns that have been fully broken down and then taken a Coy/step loss can replace that Coy breakdown or re-constitute a fully broken down Btn with one step.
Note: The US player cannot end up with more steps of any type than was present at the beginning of the scenario.

### German Replacements (Cherbourg) (34.10.2)

The German side receives six replacement steps of infantry on the GD that the Allied side declares a lull. These replacements can be absorbed by any reduced German unit on the map that can be rebuilt using Inf RePs. No more than one step may be replaced in each reduced German unit. Eliminated units may not be brought back into play.

modifications.gate: AW Scenario 4: Intermediate logistics. No Logistics Table. ADV and AmP per scenario. Simplified reinforcements — units arrive 2 GDs later than Activation List (AW (35.0), (36.0)).

appendNotes: Scenario 4 uses intermediate logistics — simpler than full rules but more involved than introductory. Check AW (35.5.0) for fuel penalties and depot rules.
Allied reinforcements arrive 2 GDs later than listed because the Strategic Map/Naval Transit Display is not used (AW (35.3.0)).

patch.appendContent:
### Scenario Four: Battle of the Hedgerows; Battle for Caen (36.0)

This scenario depicts the attempt by both the US and British AGs to expand their invasion lodgments and capture the key communication centers of St. Lo and Caen. All of the beachheads have been joined together in one continuous front and Cherbourg has been captured. All maps except for map A are used.

### Scenario Length (Hedgerows) (36.1.0)

The scenario begins on GT 79 (July 2 AM GT) and ends on GT 131 (July 19 PM GT) and is 53 turns long. The scenario may be split with players just playing one or the other of the Allied AG.

### Air Points (Hedgerows) (36.2.0)

GOSS & AW rule (20.0) applies.
Exception: Ignore all rules applying to Strategic Map assignment, air supply mission, and Strategic Interdiction. Use the sequence of play outlined in AW (35.0).

### Available Air Points (Hedgerows) (36.2.1)

• US receives 44 AP each GD.
• CW receives 40 AP each GD.
• The Allied player may not increase his air effort (AW (20.1.1c)).
• There are eight HB points available to conduct one CB mission for each AG. These missions may not be conducted on the same GD. Both CB attacks are conducted as the third attack. All available APs for both AG are halved the GD of the CB mission. The Allied side may pre-plan their Carpet Bombing prior to the start of play.
• German side receives 6 APs each GD. Use the abort procedure listed on the Air Point Availability Table.

### Naval Gunfire Support (Hedgerows) (36.3.0)

All rules pertaining to Naval Gunfire Support apply. Additionally:
• The Western TF may only roll for DD; no other naval unit is available.
• Beginning on the July 8 GD, the Eastern TF may have a maximum of 1 BB or 1 Mon, 1 CA, 2 CL and 4 DD. The CW player rolls normally, however Eastern TF may not have more naval units than listed above.

### Weather (Hedgerows) (36.4.0)

The weather on the first GT of the scenario is POvr with rain. The ground is wet. On the second GT begin rolling for variable weather.

### Logistics (Hedgerows) (36.5.0)

Use the rules outlined in AW (35.0) and follow the below procedures. The British 2nd Army and US 1st Army have one depot each. The 3rd US Army and the 1st Canadian Army may not activate in this scenario. Additional depots may not be activated.

### PSS (Hedgerows) (36.5.1)

• British 2nd Army's PSS is either Gold or Juno beach. Only one army may trace from each beach.
• US 1st Army's PSS is Omaha Beach.
• German PSS is the same as AW (16.1.0).
• Ignore all references to the Strategic Map, Embarked Units, and Landefront Cherbourg.

### ADV (Hedgerows) (36.5.2)

• Allied ADV for each is five.
• German ADV for each army is three.

### AmP (Hedgerows) (36.5.3)

• Each Allied army has five AmPs in stockpile and receives three AmPs each AM GT.
• Each German army has two AmPs in stockpile. The German side receives the following AmP each AM GT.
• One AmP each AM GT.
• If the atmospheric condition in the Eastern Sector is overcast with rain or storm the German side receives two AmPs each AM GT.

### Fuel (Hedgerows) (36.5.4)

Use the procedures in AW (35.51) & (35.5.2).

### Truck Points (Hedgerows) (36.5.5)

TP are only used in this scenario to motorize leg units.
• Each Allied army has three TPs available for motorization. Allied inherent division transport is available.
• The German side has two TPs available. These points may be assigned to either army at the beginning of each AM GT.

### Replacements (Hedgerows) (36.6.0)

A simple form of replacements is used for this scenario. Use GOSS (22.1.3).

### Allied Replacements (Hedgerows) (36.6.1)

• 21st AG receives three Inf and three armor ReP each AM GT.
• 12th AG receives four Inf and four armor ReP each AM GT.

### German Replacements (Hedgerows) (36.6.2)

• The German side receives one Inf and one armor ReP each AM GT.

modifications.gate: AW Scenario 5: Intermediate logistics. No Logistics Table. ADV and AmP per scenario. Carpet bombing may be available (AW (35.0), (37.0)).

appendNotes: Cobra features the massive Allied carpet bombing that broke the German line — check scenario rules for carpet bombing availability and restrictions (AW (37.0)).
Allied reinforcements arrive 2 GDs later than Activation List (AW (35.3.0)).

patch.appendContent:
### Scenario Five: July 25, Cobra (37.0)

This scenario highlights Omar Bradley's set-piece operation to finally break out of the hedgerows and set Patton loose behind German lines. The operation began with a massive carpet-bombing mission that annihilated large sections of the German frontline units, allowing two armored divisions and two infantry divisions to break into the thick of German defenses and grind their way through. On the British front, the 7th Armored Division and the Canadian corps fought ferociously to crack the ridgeline near Verviers and drive beyond down the road to Falaise. After just several days of action, Monty was forced to redirect his attacks elsewhere as this front was just too strongly held. All maps except for map A are used.

### Scenario Length (Cobra) (37.1.0)

The scenario begins on GT 146 (July 25 AM GT) and ends on GT 171 (August 2 PM GT) and is 55 GTs long. The scenario may be split with players just playing one or the other of the Allied armies.
• Use the sequence of play outlined in AW (35.0).
• In both the full scenario and scenario 5B the US 1st Army has just cancelled a lull (side one).

### Air Points (Cobra) (37.2.0)

GOSS & AW rule (20.0) applies.
Exception: Ignore all rules applying to Strategic Map assignment, air supply mission, and Strategic Interdiction.

### Available Air Points (Cobra) (37.2.1)

• US receives 44 AP each GD.
• CW receives 40 AP each GD.
• The Allied player may not increase his air effort (AW (20.1.1c)).
• There are eight HB points available to conduct one CB mission for each AG. These missions may not be conducted on the same GD. Both CB attacks are conducted as the third attack. All available APs for both AG are halved the GD of the CB mission. The Allied side may pre-plan their Carpet Bombing prior to the start of play.
• German side receives 6 APs each GD. Use the abort procedure listed on the Air Point Availability Table.

### Naval Gunfire Support (Cobra) (37.3.0)

All rules pertaining to Naval Gunfire Support apply. Additionally:
• The Western TF may only roll for DD; no other naval unit is available.
• The Eastern TF may have a maximum of 1 BB or 1 Mon, 1 CA, 2 CL and 4 DD. The CW player rolls normally, however Eastern TF may not have more naval units than listed above.

### Weather (Cobra) (37.4.0)

The weather on the first two GTs of the scenario is clear and dry. On the third GT begin rolling for weather.

### Logistics (Cobra) (37.5.0)

Use the rules outlined in AW (35.0) and follow the below procedures. All Allied armies have one depot each. The 2nd British, 1st Canadian, and 1st US Armies are active. Each army has one depot. HQ and depot may be placed as players' desire as long as all are in GenS.

### PSS (Cobra) (37.5.1)

• CW armies use either Gold or Juno beach. Only one army may trace from each beach.
• US 1st Army's PSS is Omaha Beach.
• When 3rd Army moves on to Map B, the 3rd Army HQ and one depot is placed on a primary road hex within 24 hexes of B2701. The depot may never move. The HQ may move then next GT.
• German PSS is the same as AW (16.1.0).
• Ignore all references to the Strategic Map, Embarked Units, and Landefront Cherbourg.

### ADV (Cobra) (37.5.2)

• Allied ADV for each is five.
• German ADV for each army is three.

### AmP (Cobra) (37.5.3)

• Each Allied army has seven AmPs in stockpile and receives four AmPs each AM GT.
• Each German army has two AmPs in stockpile. The German side receives the following AmP each AM GT.
• One AmP each AM GT.
• If the atmospheric condition in the Eastern Sector is overcast with rain or storm the German side receives two AmPs each AM GT.

### Fuel (Cobra) (37.5.4)

Use the procedures in AW (35.51) & (35.5.2).

### Truck Points (Cobra) (37.5.5)

TP are only used in this scenario to motorize leg units.
• Each Allied army has three TPs available for motorization. Allied inherent division transport is available.
• The German side has two TPs available. These points may be assigned to either army at the beginning of each AM GT.

### Replacements (Cobra) (37.6.0)

A simple form of replacements is used for this scenario.

### Allied Replacements (Cobra) (37.6.1)

• 21st AG has five Inf and five Arm ReP available. It receives three Inf and three armor ReP each AM GT.
• 12th AG six Inf and six Arm ReP available. It receives four Inf and four armor ReP each AM GT.

### German Replacements (Cobra) (37.6.2)

The German side has one Inf and one Arm ReP available and receives one Inf and one armor ReP each AM GT. These may be distributed to either army.

modifications.gate: AW Scenario 6: Intermediate logistics with full Airborne and Amphibious modules active. June 6-12. Use SoP from AW (35.1.0). Naval units available starting June 7 AM or after all beaches cleared (AW (38.0)).

appendNotes: This is the full D-Day experience — Airborne and Amphibious stages on June 6, then normal GTs with simplified logistics through June 12 (AW (38.1.0)).
The scenario can be split — play just the US (12th AG) or CW (21st AG) side separately (AW (38.11.0)).
Naval units are NOT available on June 6 (supporting landings). Starting June 7 AM, roll for naval unit availability (AW (38.3.0)).

patch.appendContent:
### Scenario Six: D-Day, The Sixth of June (38.0)

Well, you finally reached it, the Invasion scenario. The Americans storm ashore on Utah and Omaha beaches after dropping two airborne divisions on the Cotentin Peninsula. The Commonwealth forces land at three nearly adjacent beach areas: Gold, Juno, and Sword, with an airdrop of the 6th Airborne Division just to the east of the Orne River.

**ATTENTION: YOU HAVE READ ALL RULES REQUIRED FOR SCENARIO FIVE!!**

### Scenario Length (38.1.0)

The scenario begins on the June 6 AM GT and ends on the June 12 night GT. The June 6 AM GT begins with the Airborne Assault Segment of the SoP. The scenario may be split with players just playing one or the other of the Allied armies. Maps A, B and C are needed for this scenario.
• Use the sequence of play outlined in AW (35.1.0) and the Airborne Assault and Amphibious Modules.
• After completing the Airborne Assault Stage and the AM Amphibious Assault Stage, normal GT play starts on the Jun 6 AM GT.
• Unless noted in the scenario all GOSS and AW rules are in effect.

### Air Points (38.2.0)

Ignore all rules applying to Strategic Map assignment, air supply mission, and Strategic Interdiction.

### Available Air Points (38.2.1)

• On the June 6 GD the US player has 18 AP available. Beginning the June 7 GD he has 44 AP available each GD.
• On the June 6 GD the CW player has 16 AP available. Beginning the June 7 GD he has 40 AP available each GD.
• Do not use HB or ATP ignore all rules pertaining to these points.
• The German side does not have any AP available on the June 6 GD. Beginning the June 7 GD he has six APs available each GD. Use the abort procedure listed on the Air Point Availability Table.

### Naval Gunfire Support (38.3.0)

Naval units are not available on June 6 (they are supporting the landings).
• Starting June 7 AM GT or on the first GT after all beaches are cleared use AW (11.9.0).
• If any beach is not cleared of German units on the June 7 AM GT, the AG that has any beach that is not cleared does not roll for naval units. Each GT until all beaches are cleared, that AG NGS support on any not cleared beach uses the same procedures as called for in AW (42.0), the June 6 PM GT. NGS is not available on the June 6 Night GT.

### Weather (38.4.0)

Use the historical weather listed in AW (19.2.1). Beginning the June 8 AM GT begin rolling for random weather conditions.

### June 6 Special Rules (38.5.0)

The following special rules apply on June 6.

### Allied Traffic Jams (38.5.1)

All Allied Mech movement allowances are halved. Strat Road movement is not allowed.

### Allied Movement Halts (38.5.2)

During the June 6 AM GT, Allied units ignore all movement halts generated by German leg units. The MP cost to move adjacent to German leg units is ignored.

### Allied PA Restriction (38.5.3)

The Allies may not enter PA mode or conduct PA attacks on June 6.

### Allied Commandos (38.5.4)

Commando special assaults may be used (AW 4.5.6a).

### June 6 Landing Sequence (38.5.5)

Use the procedures outlined in AW (42.0) and AW (21.6.2).

### German Activation (38.5.6)

Units are activated either by proximity to Allied units or on a timed basis. Refer to the German Activation List for restrictions on individual units or formations.

### German MA on June 6 (38.5.7)

On the June 6 AM and PM GTs, all German units that begin a Movement Phase within six hexes of a Beach landing hex (hexes with triangle) halve their MA.

### Logistics (38.6.0)

All of the rules pertaining to GenS are in effect (AW 15.0 & 16.0). The logistic procedures in AW (35.0) are used.
Exception: Ignore all references to the Strategic Map and Embarked Units.

### Ammo Delivery Value (38.6.1)

The ADV for each side is listed below. Adjust army and corps ADV based on the number of formations assigned and the use of AmP. Do not use the Logistic Table.

### Allied ADV & AmP (38.6.1a)

• June 6 GD the ADV for each Allied corps is five.
• June 7 GD, until the end of the scenario, the ADV for each Allied corps is three.
• The British 6th AB Div, US 82nd and 101st AB Div each has an ADV of two.
• Each AG receives two AmPs each GT. They may be divided between the corps as desired.

### German ADV & AmP (38.6.1b)

• June 6 ADV is seven. WN, and StP are exempt from ammo depletion throughout the scenario.
• Beginning on the June 7 AM GT the German ADV is five.
• The German side has two AmPs in stockpile. The German side receives the following AmP each AM GT.
  • One AmP each AM GT.
  • If the atmospheric condition in the Eastern Sector is overcast with rain or storm the German side receives two AmPs each AM GT.

### Fuel (38.6.2)

Use the procedures in AW (35.5.1) & (35.5.2).

### Truck Points (38.6.3)

TP are only used to motorize leg units in this scenario.
• The Allied side has 1 TP per AG.
• The German side has two TPs available.

### Replacements (38.7.0)

Replacements are not received until the June 8 AM GT. Neither side has any ReP in stockpile. AW & GOSS (22.0) applies.

modifications.gate: AW Campaign: Full campaign from June 6 onward. Simplified logistics with optional full logistic and strategic rules (AW (39.0)). All Airborne and Amphibious modules active on June 6.

appendNotes: The campaign game can use either simplified or full logistics — players choose before starting. Full logistics add the Logistics Table, Strategic Map, and Naval Transit Display (AW (39.4.0)).
Optional second airborne assault (1st British/Polish AB or second US airborne drop) may be available later in the campaign (AW (39.6.0)).

patch.appendContent:
### Campaign Game (39.0)

This is the whole shebang!!! All five Battlefield Maps are used. Players may choose to use the full logistic and strategic rules, or may choose to use the simplified rules.

### Game Length (39.1.0)

The campaign game begins on the June 6 AM GT and ends on the Aug 22 PM GT. The June 6 AM GT begins with the Airborne Assault and Amphibious Assault Segment of the SoP. It is highly recommended that players use the full Logistic and Strategic rules.

### Simplified Rules (39.2.0)

The simplified logistic rules for the full campaign were never fully play tested, so beware that play balance could be affected. For those players who choose not to use the full logistic and strategic rules, use the sequence of play and logistic rules outlined in AW (35.0). Ignore all references to the Strategic Map, Naval Transit Displays, Logistic Table, and procedures for determining fuel/AmP unless mentioned below. All remaining GOSS and AW rules apply, except as follows:

### Air & Naval Availability (39.2.1)

AW (38.2.0) & (38.3.0) apply. Each GD a carpet bombing mission is conducted, Allied AP availability is zero. ATPs are available for standard GOSS resupply starting June 12 AM GT. The Allied side may increase AP availability but may only be changed using the below two methods.
• The US player automatically increases his total available AP by 16 on the AM GT after Cherbourg is fully operational. This is a permanent increase with no penalties.
• On August 1 through August 15 GDs, the total available AP available for the US player is reduced by 20. This applies to the total US AP, including additions.

### Logistics (39.2.3)

Use AW (15.0) & (16.0) for GenS path lengths and activation of Allied army HQ and depots. Use AW (35.5.0) for determining fuel restrictions. ADV is adjusted for support and creating or expending ADV only. Ignore all rules pertaining to the Naval Transit Display, Strategic Map, and Logistic Table. ADV, AmP, and TP values are given below.

### ADV, AmP, & Trucks (39.2.4)

ADV, AmP, and Truck Point values for the campaign game under simplified rules.

### German ADV, AmP, & Trucks (39.2.4a)

June 6 GD:
• ADV is seven.
• Start with two AmPs in stockpile.
• One TP is available for motorization.
June 7 GD:
• ADV is five.
• Two TP are available for motorization.
June 8 GD:
• ADV is four.
• One AmP is received each AM GT. If the atmospheric condition in the Eastern Sector is overcast with rain or storm two AmPs are received each AM GT.
• Three TP are available for motorization.
July 1 GD:
• ADV is five.
• Two AmPs are received each AM GT. If the atmospheric condition in the Eastern Sector is overcast with rain or storm three AmPs are received each AM GT.
• Three TP are available for motorization.
August 1 GD:
• ADV is three.
• AmP may only be gained by lowering army ADV.
• Two TP are available for motorization.

### Allied ADV, AmP, & Trucks (39.2.4b)

June 6 GD:
• ADV is five.
• No AmP in stockpile.
• No TP available for motorization.
June 7 GD:
• ADV is three. Airborne Divisions have an ADV of two.
• Each AG receives two AmPs.
• Each Corps has one TP available for motorization.
June 11 GD:
• Same as June 7, except Airborne Divisions are assigned to corps.
• Values do not change until an AG has an active army HQ, then that AG uses the below values.
Army HQ Activation:
• ADV is 5.
• Each active army HQ receives three AmPs each AM GT.
• Each active army HQ has three TPs available for motorization.
• Values do not change until an AG has an active depot, then that AG uses the below values.
Depot Activation:
Each army HQ with an active depot:
• has an ADV of six.
• Receives four AmPs each AM GT.
• Has three TPs available for motorization.

### Full Logistic & Strategic Rules (39.4.0)

All rules GOSS and AW Logistic/Strategic Rules are in effect.

### June 6 GD Special Rules (39.4.1)

AW (38.5.0) applies. Each Allied AG has one-half of their total AP available. The German do not have any AP available.

### Logistics (39.4.2)

All GOSS logistic and GenS rules apply except as modified by AW (15.0) & (16.0).

### Reinforcements (39.3.0)

AW (35.0) applies. Use the arrival schedule in (38.8.0) for Allied reinforcements from June 6 to June 10. Use the Allied Activation Lists (AW 40.0) beginning June 11. German units activate using AW (40.3.0). Use the random arrival rule (AW 35.3.1).
